user.title: Connect to HyperHDR
user.description: Enter the address of your HyperHDR server.

data_description.host: IP address or hostname of the HyperHDR server. If HyperHDR sits behind a reverse proxy (e.g. Traefik) that terminates TLS, enter that proxy's hostname here and enable "Use SSL" below.
data_description.port: The JSON API port (default 8090). If you're going through a reverse proxy, this is the proxy's exposed port, not necessarily 8090.
data_description.use_ssl: Enable if HyperHDR is only reachable over wss:// (typically via a TLS-terminating reverse proxy). Most direct-LAN setups leave this off.
data_description.verify_ssl: Disable only for a self-signed or otherwise untrusted certificate (e.g. many home reverse-proxy setups). Has no effect unless "Use SSL" is enabled.

ssdp_confirm.title: HyperHDR discovered
ssdp_confirm.description: A HyperHDR server was found on your network. Confirm (or adjust) its connection details to add it.

data_description.host: IP address or hostname of the HyperHDR server. If HyperHDR sits behind a reverse proxy (e.g. Traefik) that terminates TLS, enter that proxy's hostname here and enable "Use SSL" below.
data_description.port: The JSON API port (default 8090). If you're going through a reverse proxy, this is the proxy's exposed port, not necessarily 8090.
data_description.use_ssl: Enable if HyperHDR is only reachable over wss:// (typically via a TLS-terminating reverse proxy). Most direct-LAN setups leave this off.
data_description.verify_ssl: Disable only for a self-signed or otherwise untrusted certificate (e.g. many home reverse-proxy setups). Has no effect unless "Use SSL" is enabled.

auth.title: API token required
auth.description: This HyperHDR server requires an API token. Create one in HyperHDR's web UI under **Network Services → API Access**, then paste it below. (Requesting a new token from within Home Assistant isn't supported yet -- use HyperHDR's own UI.)

data_description.token: The token string shown after creating/approving it in HyperHDR's Network Services panel.

admin.title: Admin access (optional)
admin.description: Provide HyperHDR's admin password to let Home Assistant start/stop instances and use other admin-gated features (e.g. live preview). Leave this blank to skip -- you can still control colors, effects, and adjustments without it.

data_description.admin_password: HyperHDR's admin password (set in Network Services → User Interface Access). Leave empty if you don't want Home Assistant to have admin access.

reauth_confirm.title: Reauthenticate HyperHDR
reauth_confirm.description: Home Assistant lost access to this HyperHDR server. Provide an updated API token and/or admin password.

data_description.token: Leave blank if the server no longer requires a token.
data_description.admin_password: Leave blank to leave the previously configured admin password unchanged.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure HyperHDR
reconfigure.description: Update this HyperHDR server's connection details.

data_description.host: IP address or hostname of the HyperHDR server. If HyperHDR sits behind a reverse proxy (e.g. Traefik) that terminates TLS, enter that proxy's hostname here and enable "Use SSL" below.
data_description.port: The JSON API port (default 8090). If you're going through a reverse proxy, this is the proxy's exposed port, not necessarily 8090.
data_description.use_ssl: Enable if HyperHDR is only reachable over wss:// (typically via a TLS-terminating reverse proxy). Most direct-LAN setups leave this off.
data_description.verify_ssl: Disable only for a self-signed or otherwise untrusted certificate (e.g. many home reverse-proxy setups). Has no effect unless "Use SSL" is enabled.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to HyperHDR. Check the host, port, and SSL settings.
error.unknown: Unexpected error. Check the Home Assistant logs for details.

abort.already_configured: This HyperHDR server is already configured.
abort.already_in_progress: This HyperHDR server is already being configured.
abort.cannot_connect: Could not determine the discovered device's address.
abort.wrong_device: This address belongs to a different HyperHDR server than the one being reconfigured.

data_description.default_priority: The HyperHDR priority (1-253) Home Assistant uses when setting colors/effects. Lower numbers win over higher numbers on HyperHDR's priority stack.
data_description.hidden_effects: Effects to exclude from the effect list entity (e.g. ones you never use). Effects already hidden remain selectable here even if HyperHDR isn't currently reporting them (e.g. while disconnected).
data_description.request_timeout: How long to wait for a response to a single command before treating it as failed.
data_description.heartbeat: How long the connection may sit idle before Home Assistant sends a lightweight request to keep it alive and detect a dead connection sooner.
data_description.stale_timeout: Force a reconnect if nothing has been received from the server for this long.

set_color.name: Set color
set_color.description: Set a solid color at a priority on a HyperHDR instance.

priority.name: Priority
priority.description: HyperHDR priority to set the color at (1-253). Defaults to this integration's configured default priority when omitted.

duration.name: Duration
duration.description: How long to show the color, in seconds. Leave blank to show it indefinitely.

set_effect.name: Set effect
set_effect.description: Start a HyperHDR effect at a priority on a HyperHDR instance.

effect.name: Effect
effect.description: The exact name of the effect to start, as reported by HyperHDR.

priority.name: Priority
priority.description: HyperHDR priority to start the effect at (1-253). Defaults to this integration's configured default priority when omitted.

duration.name: Duration
duration.description: How long to run the effect, in seconds. Leave blank to run it indefinitely.

clear.name: Clear priority
clear.description: Clear a priority (or every priority) on a HyperHDR instance.

priority.name: Priority
priority.description: The priority to clear. Use -1 to clear every priority.
